rationale.R1.1: The section opens with a clear contribution claim—inter-agent dialogue drives compositional accuracy—but the conclusion retracts this in favor of chain-of-thought scaffolding; reviewing the section centrally requires reconstructing and evaluating whether a coherent thesis exists.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing inconsistency: the opening frames role-conditioned dialogue as the key driver, but the ablation shows a single Reasoner with passive templates recovers 4.9 of 5.6 points, causing the conclusion to pivot to a different causal story; this internal contradiction is the most critical reviewable defect.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are made (64.3% vs 58.7%, +9.1 vs +1.8 on chain depth, 4.9/5.6 ablation recovery); a review must verify these figures are cited consistently and that the conclusions drawn from them are warranted by the numbers shown.
rationale.R1.6: A general 'review' delegation includes prose-level assessment; the section is largely readable but employs softening language ('suggest', 'principal driver') that partially obscures a substantive claim retraction, warranting attention.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review prioritize technical/scientific validity (logical consistency of the contribution claim, evidence-claim alignment in the ablation) or writing quality (prose clarity, grammar, sentence-level polish)?
